Early game Salv gets enough from passive Furnance gain, and 3 cost, and most importantly, stalling, mob fights. For Mob fights, you can basically stall with leaving on enemy alive, and just shield back to full health and max furnace. For Mini Bosses, you just enter and set up Nuke turn with Max furnance and your good. For Bosses, enter with max Furnace, first burst turn, then stall with your other Awakeners to build up Furnace. Use your "End of Suffering" for Middle Option if possible. Left Option if its the bosses burst turn, and NEVER use right option for boss fights, unless you can duplicate his "End of Suffering".
For Endgame, you ideally run, Caecus, Goliath, Thais.
with Lone Seed.

Endgame Salv is all about the big CON stat as this is what Salvador "End of Suffering" scales off of. Goliath and Caecus have the one of the highest stats in the game, thus providing the big CON for a higher Max Hp pool. Goliath can further your burst turn with Exalt, and "Law of Strong". Caecus can apply Vuln with Exalt, and give you that bit of shielding needed with healing. Thais is there to dupe Salv's "End of Suffering". This is were playstyles diverge. You want to still stall with one mob enemy to max furnace and heal, that remains the same. However, for boss fights, you want to Thais's Exalt and Lone Seed, Salv's "End of Suffering", and make copies. Then with those copies, you flex whatever option is needed. Most of the time following the order of maxing furnace to Nuke and then refueling furnace, or shielding and then refueling. There are cases where you go Right Option, and the full heal with Furnace, and then refuel as well.
And of course, Furnace scales with Max Hp

For Tawil/Inferno/Ryker/Karen:
I would not run Mono Tawil. Its a team that wants heavy investment to be able to infinitely cycle your deck. It's very "endgamy" and requires more investment then this. I would recommend replacing Karen for Caecus, and go Aequor Tawil. Having access to Aequor Cards from Tawil and Tentacle Stance will give you much survivability.

For Caecus/Murphy/Sanga/Nautila:
I can see your going Nautila + Sanga Burst Exalt. This is fine until you need OE Sanga and need to slot in Winkle for her Aliemus hungry ass. This team works fine and would only need to replace Caecus with Celeste or Karen.

For Cantena/Thais/Ramona/24:
This team is kind of weird. I don't have a through understanding of Caro 24, but from what I read from her Caro form, she creates Embryos and fuels Crimsum Furnace. Ramona is Silver Key Gen, but can be replaced with Aigis, a better Caro Silver Key Generator. Thais works with Cantena, perfectly. I would personally replace 24 with a defensive option such as Ogier.
^ Actuallys since you have a Horla, I would have Horla last slot in the Cantena team

I'm curious, what is the "heavy investment" Tawil requires? <:thinkernotes:1352321034937958440>
And is it specifically "mono tawil" (I guess this means tawil is only chaos on that team) or any setup with tawil?
Mono Tawil indicates that only Tawil will be playing any cards

For Tulu/Goliath/Castor/Horla:
Castor is a unit that can carry almost any team by himself, so he doesnt necessarily need anyone that furthers his damage. Aequor Ultra is also a team that is very uncommon, but I use it with Castor as well. Tulu, and Goliath are both Exalts that are used for Burst Turn, which can work, however, I would recommend replacing one of them for a defensive option. Goliath has more flexibility then Tulu, so I would recommend replacing Tulu. Now we have Castor, and Goliath. From here, you don't have much more Aequor options, but Celeste can work for more defensive. However if we lean more into Ultra, we can go Winkle, Erica. If you want to go Mono Ultra, I would recommend replacing Goliath with Jenkin.

ok so to summarize base SSR chance is 3%, whenever you proc the SSR its a 66% of an off rate wheel/awakener and a 33% of the rate up, then its a 50/50 between the wheel and awakener, if you roll an off rate the next one is guaranteed to be a rate up, after two of the same rate up (awakener or wheel) the next rate up is guaranteed to be the other one (which in most people’s cases it the awakener
if anyone is lurking and saw me say something wrong please correct me

worst odds of E0 are around 180ish pulls+2 signature wheels which can actually be quite good biggest example being Thais
whereas normal rateup is 90 pulls worst odds but no wheels

I would say play whatever Archetype/Mechanic/Realm/Awakener interests you a s beginner. In the beginning, you have the option of playing ANY characters, which later on, you can’t. If you play only what’s “best” then you will have zero experience with any  other teams. <:cry2:1356068126894194758>

Try to build a team that follows a certain Archetype(Tentacle Slaps Spam/Infinite Multi Hits/Hard Burst). If possible, you want two Awakeners that deal damage, and two awakeners that are support or keep you alive.

It gives more damage, but it doesn’t matter if you have enough damage. Its ok a problem if you lack damage. Otherwise, you can find random sources of Vuln from Relics, Orisons, Curses. If you want something more consistent, you can change your Posse. Other then that, Vuln isn’t neccessary if you don’t need it. <:point:1356053112292511815> <:slick:1356053160531198064>
